title: Title 1
description:
This stained-glass window presents a vivid and reverent portrayal of the Holy Spirit, drawing the eye immediately to the descending dove and the rising flame beneath it. The dove, long recognized as the biblical sign of the Spirit, recalls the moment of our Lord’s baptism: “He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming to rest on him” (Matthew 3:16, ESV). Here, the dove is not merely symbolic but confessional—bearing witness to the truth that the Spirit rests upon Christ and reveals Him as the Son of God.

Beneath the dove, the flame burns with layered hues of gold, red, and orange, evoking the event of Pentecost: “And divided tongues as of fire appeared to them and rested on each one of them” (Acts 2:3, ESV). The fire signifies not destruction, but divine presence and purification. It proclaims that the same Spirit who descended upon Christ is given to His Church, igniting faith and sustaining believers through the Word.

The interplay of light through the glass—bright yellows and warm tones surrounding the central image—suggests illumination. This reflects the Spirit’s ongoing work of enlightening hearts and minds. As our Lord promises, “When the Helper comes… he will bear witness about me” (John 15:26, ESV). The Spirit does not speak on His own authority but directs all attention to Jesus Christ—His person, His saving work, and His promises.

The geometric patterns framing the dove and flame create a sense of order and movement, guiding the viewer inward to the center. This visual movement mirrors the Spirit’s work through the Means of Grace—drawing sinners to Christ through the preached Word, Holy Baptism, and the Lord’s Supper. The Spirit gathers, enlightens, and sanctifies the whole Christian Church on earth, keeping it with Jesus Christ in the one true faith.

Set within the sanctuary, this window serves not only as art but as proclamation. It confesses that the Holy Spirit is actively at work among God’s people, continually testifying to who Jesus is: the crucified and risen Savior. In every beam of light passing through this glass, the faithful are reminded that the Spirit still comes—bringing Christ to His people and His people to Christ.
